page: Amazon Web Services im4gn.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores running on the arm64 architecture at 2.5 GHz. The instance features 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It is equipped with 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and software compilation via Clang scoring in the top 25%. The architecture demonstrates high efficiency in cached memory bandwidth, reaching 3782.92 GB/sec for small blocks, and excels in high-throughput operations, as evidenced by 72.61 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 46.08 GB/sec in static web serving. However, single-threaded performance is a notable tradeoff, with single-core Geekbench and compression tasks scoring in the lower percentiles. Large-block memory writes and LLM inference tasks generally perform in the average tier.

Utilizing custom ARM-based Graviton processors allows this server type to offer high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for storage-intensive workloads compared to traditional x86 alternatives. The combination of massive local NVMe SSD capacity, high baseline network bandwidth, and strong multi-core throughput makes the im4gn.16xlarge highly suitable for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, high-throughput web serving, and distributed data-store workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 cores on the arm64 architecture, running at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of memory, 30,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps under the Nitro hypervisor. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, particularly in Redis operations, static web serving, and software compilation, though single-threaded performance and LLM inference speeds represent clear tradeoffs. The custom ARM architecture delivers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive deployments. This server is optimized for high-throughput databases, caching layers, and distributed storage workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services im4gn.16xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 64 Graviton cores, 256 GB RAM, 30 TB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: An arm64 storage-optimized instance featuring thirty terabytes of local NVMe SSD storage and high-bandwidth networking for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores on arm64 architecture
30,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
256.0 GB of system memory and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong multi-core performance in Redis and static web serving benchmarks
Best suited for large-scale databases, caching layers, and distributed storage
